blocks.title: 📘 PEDIR & OFRECER · can / could / would
blocks.intro: En el trabajo pedimos y ofrecemos cosas todo el día. Usamos modales + verbo base. Repasa y practica.

cells.body:
Can you help me?
Could you send it? (más cortés)
Can I have your number?

I'll call the client.
Shall I help you?
Would you like a coffee?

Sure! / Of course.
No problem.
Sorry, I can't right now.

verbo base: Can you to send?
Can you send?
could > can (más formal)

blocks.ladderLabel: Escalera de cortesía — de directo a muy cortés:
blocks.note: Cómo funciona: elegí respuestas 💬, uní funciones 🔗 y escucha llamadas 🎧. ✓ CHECK en cada uno.

blocks.tag: un día de llamadas y chats
blocks.title: ★ A BUSY DAY AT THE OFFICE
blocks.intro: Marcos atiende una llamada de una clienta y ayuda a Sofia por chat. Todo el día pide y ofrece ayuda.

cast.sp: la team lead
una clienta

blocks.title: ELEGÍ LA RESPUESTA
blocks.intro: Alguien te pide u ofrece algo. Elegí la respuesta correcta.

lines.html: Could you send me the report today?

options: Of course, I'll send it this afternoon.

lines.html: Shall I help you with the slides?

options: Yes, please, that would be great.

lines.html: Can you book a meeting room for ten o'clock?

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · A CALL FROM A CLIENT
blocks.desc: Marcos atiende una llamada. La clienta quiere hablar con Sofia.
blocks.speakers: 🗣 Marcos + Emma

questions.q: Who does Emma want to speak to?
Why can't she speak to her now?
Marcos offers to take a message.

speaking.prompt: You answer the phone; the caller wants your busy boss. What do you say? Use Can I…? and I'll….
speaking.starter: I'm sorry, she's in a meeting. Can I take a message? I'll ask her to…

transcript.html: Good morning, English for All, this is Marcos speaking. How can I help you?
Hi, this is Emma from Bright Company. Could I speak to Sofia, please?
I'm sorry, Sofia is in a meeting right now. Can I take a message?
Yes, please. Could you ask her to call me back this afternoon?
Of course. Can I have your number?
Sure. And could you tell her it's about the new project?
No problem, Emma. I'll give her the message. Would you like her to email you too?
Yes, please. That would be great. Thank you so much!

blocks.title: UNÍ FUNCIÓN Y FRASE
blocks.intro: ¿Para qué sirve cada frase? Tocá la función y luego la frase.

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · A CHAT AT WORK
blocks.desc: Sofia le pide ayuda a Marcos con un informe.
blocks.speakers: 🗣 Sofia + Marcos

questions.q: What does Sofia ask Marcos to check?
What does Marcos offer to do as well?
options: Fix the formatting and add the logo

questions.q: Sofia wants it sent to the client before five.

speaking.prompt: A colleague is busy. Offer to help with two things. Use I'll… and Shall I…?
speaking.starter: Don't worry, I'll check the numbers now. Shall I also…?

transcript.html: Marcos, can you help me with the report? I'm very busy today.
Sure, Sofia. What do you need?
Could you check the numbers on page two? I think there's a mistake.
No problem. I'll check them now. Do you want me to fix the formatting too?
Yes, please. And could you send it to the client before five?
Of course. Shall I add the new logo as well?
Good idea! Thank you so much, Marcos.
Happy to help. I'll send it to you first to review.

blocks.title: ✍️ A WORK CHAT
blocks.intro: Escribe un chat de trabajo corto (5–6 líneas). Pide 2 cosas y ofrece 1:
checklist: Responde a un pedido (Sure! / Of course / Sorry, I can't…)
Usa una palabra de chat/teléfono (send, attach, call back)
blocks.example: Hi Ana! Could you send me the report before lunch? And can you attach the photos too? — Sure, no problem! I'll send everything now. Shall I also forward it to the client? — Yes, please, that would be great. Thank you!
blocks.hw: Escribe tu chat y graba un audio leyéndolo (las dos voces). Envíame ambos por WhatsApp.
